Suzlon share price rebounds from day’s low after announcement of this growth plan
India's services sector growth could stall as downside risks build
top_bearish: Top Gainers & Losers on June 3: TCS, Coforge, PB Fintech, JM Financial, Tata Elxsi, Thermax among top losers today
Sensex rebounds 850 points from day's low to end 300 points down: 10 key highlights from Indian stock market today

articles.title: Top Gainers & Losers on June 3: TCS, Coforge, PB Fintech, JM Financial, Tata Elxsi, Thermax among top losers today
articles.summary: The Indian stock market fell on June 3, with the Nifty 50 down 0.36% and the Sensex down 0.54%. Rising crude oil prices and escalating Middle East tensions contributed to the decline, impacting investor confidence and market sentiment.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Sensex rebounds 850 points from day's low to end 300 points down: 10 key highlights from Indian stock market today
articles.summary: The Indian stock market resumed its losing run after a one-day hiatus on Wednesday, 3 June, although it closed significantly off the day's low, signalling some low-level buying by investors.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Google's $80 billion move is a ‘wake-up call’ for Indian Inc, says Uday Kotak on X
articles.summary: Uday Kotak, in a post on X, pointed to a recent announcement by Google, which despite being cash surplus, unveiled an additional capital raise of $80 billion.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Borosil Renewables shares surge 10% as government extends solar glass import duties
articles.summary: Borosil Renewables shares rose 10% to ₹549.90 after the government imposed new duties on solar glass imports from Malaysia. This marks a significant gain, with nearly 12% increase over two sessions. The measures aim to strengthen India’s solar supply chain and protect local manufacturers.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Suzlon share price rebounds from day’s low after announcement of this growth plan
articles.summary: Suzlon share price has fallen 3% in one month and has rallied over 32% in three months. The stock has gained 3% in six months, but has fallen 20% in one year. Suzlon shares have delivered multibagger returns of 393% in three years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Meesho lock-in expiry looms. Time to be cautious as ₹60,000-crore worth of stocks to be tradable?
articles.summary: Analysts advise caution while approaching Meesho stock in the run-up to the lock-in expiry, as past instances signal selling pressure around this event.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India's services sector growth could stall as downside risks build
articles.summary: Strong domestic demand lifted services activity in May, but elevated costs, softer confidence and geopolitical risks could test the sector's momentum in the months ahead.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Anthropic IPO: AI giant confidentially files IPO; 5 key things to know
articles.summary: Anthropic has confidentially filed for an IPO in the US. The filing, which does not disclose financial details, could influence other AI companies to pursue public listings and reflects Anthropic's rapid growth and high valuation in the industry.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Oracle stock price falls after 30% surge in three sessions; what’s next?
articles.summary: Oracle share price had surged nearly 30% over the previous three trading sessions. The stock has gained 18% over the past six months, while rising 25% YTD. On a one-year basis, Oracle stock price has delivered returns of approximately 45%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO to open on 5 June. Here's what GMP signals ahead of offer launch
articles.summary: Hexagon Nutrition IPO consists entirely of an offer for sale (OFS) of up to 30.86 million equity shares, with no fresh issue component. Here's what the GMP signals ahead of the offer launch.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: OpenAI, Anthropic are minting billions. Why isn't AI boosting Indian IT stocks like Infosys, TCS? Samir Arora answers
articles.summary: Investor Samir Arora questions why enterprises are spending billions on AI models from providers while still relying on IT services firms for implementation. He highlights a disconnect between reported AI revenues and claims of needing extensive external support for deployment.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Auto sector Q4 results review: Volumes, price hikes aid revenue growth, profits beat; M&M, TVS Motors top picks
articles.summary: Auto sector Q4 results review: Revenue of auto OEMs, excluding Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles, grew 24.1% YoY in the March quarter, driven by more than 20% YoY increase in the tractors, LCV, two-wheelers and MHCV segments’ volumes, and teens growth in the PV and LCV segments’ volumes.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Tata Sons, Jio, NSE listings can change the market. Here's why Deepak Shenoy thinks India needs more IPOs
articles.summary: Deepak Shenoy advocates for more company listings in India to absorb growing domestic capital and improve market liquidity. He warns against blaming SIPs for market issues and emphasizes the importance of long-term investment opportunities.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI MPC meeting begins today: Will RBI announce a rate hike as Middle East conflict threatens to drive inflation?
articles.summary: According to wealth management firm DSP Mutual Fund, the central bank is unlikely to announce a rate hike, instead, the bank is likely to follow a step-by-step sequence before pulling the trigger on rates to defend the currency.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Small-cap stock Shreeji Shipping Global shows resilience against stock market crash
articles.summary: Shreeji Shipping Global secures exclusive rights for handling dry bulk and liquid cargo at Karanja Creek, effective February 2026. The Tonnage Tax Scheme approval from the Income Tax Department is expected to enhance tax efficiency and support long-term growth.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: NMDC has twin boosts of pricing and firm demand
articles.summary: NMDC’s shares have gained about 16% so far in 2026, and trade at an enterprise value of 6-6.5 times its FY27 estimated Ebitda. While the price trend looks favourable currently, the medium-term trajectory may not be very favourable.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Ruchit Jain of Motilal Oswal suggests Premier Energies, Varun Beverages shares to buy for the short term
articles.summary: Indian equity benchmarks fell sharply due to geopolitical tensions, rising crude oil prices, and foreign fund outflows. The Sensex dropped 1,000 pts and the Nifty 50 fell 300 pts, with investors cautious amid US-Iran negotiations and proposed tariffs on imports from several countries.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Beyond ethanol and biogas, Praj needs to prove its growth case
articles.summary: While the green technology pioneer is beating the market slump on policy tailwinds, analysts warn near-term earnings depend on turning order visibility into hard revenue.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: TCS, Infosys to Coforge: IT stocks crash up to 9% after three-day rally; what's behind the selloff?
articles.summary: IT stocks fell sharply as profit-booking set in. Analysts highlight the impact of AI on traditional business models and caution against high valuations in a low-growth environment, leading to diminished foreign institutional investor interest.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Last day to buy Tata Group company Trent shares ahead of its first-ever bonus issue record date; do you own?
articles.summary: Trent shares are in focus as June 4 is the record date for the company's first bonus issue of 1:2. Investors must hold shares by this date to qualify. The company plans to issue 17.77 crore equity shares, capitalizing ₹17.77 crore from its reserves.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: IndusInd Bank share price falls over 3% after report of whistleblower complaint to RBI, PMO
articles.summary: The complaint has reportedly been submitted to multiple authorities, including the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO), the Reserve Bank of India (RBI), the Serious Fraud Investigation Office (SFIO), the National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA), and other regulatory agencies.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Metals Q4 results: Strong operational performance sustains amid elevated metal prices; Hindalco, APL Apollo top picks
articles.summary: Metals and mining sector showed resilience in Q4 FY26, supported by high commodity prices and strong demand. Most companies met or surpassed expectations, with APL Apollo Tubes and Hindalco receiving 'Buy' ratings from Axis Direct, highlighting their robust performance and strategic growth plans.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: From $120 to $95: Crude oil prices surged 70% during the US-Iran war. Can Brent crude price drop to $80?
articles.summary: Crude oil prices have surged due to US-Iran tensions, but historical trends suggest corrections of 30-40% are common after geopolitical spikes. Analysts warn that supply restorations are crucial for price stabilization, while the market remains sensitive to Middle East developments.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: NHPC share price rises 5% as OFS opens for retail investors. Should you participate or not?
articles.summary: NHPC stock began the day at ₹75.56 per share, up from its previous close of ₹72.29 on Tuesday. Here are all the details about the OFS, along with the experts' opinion on whether retail investors should participate or not.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vodafone Idea share price gains despite stock market crash, jumps 40% in one month. More upside left?
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea share price has gained nearly 40% in one month and has risen over 21% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis. The telecom stock has rallied 34% in six month and has jumped 108% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50 tanks 300 points, Sensex crashes 1150 points; why is Indian market falling today? Explained with 5 reasons
articles.summary: Stock market crash: Following escalation in the US-Iran war and soaring crude oil prices, key indices of Dalal Street are under intense selling pressure, shedding over 1% each.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Rajnandini Fashion share price makes flat debut, lists at ₹63, same as SME IPO price
articles.summary: Rajnandini Fashion India debuted at ₹63 on the BSE SME, meeting expectations with a grey market premium of ₹6. The IPO was oversubscribed by 203.74 times, with significant interest from retail and institutional investors. The company aims to raise ₹13.85 crore for business growth.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Chinese Investors Exit Hong Kong Stocks as AI Woos Money Onshore
articles.summary: Chinese investors are exiting Hong Kong‑listed stocks in record numbers, highlighting waning appetite as mainland AI shares broaden their appeal.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vedanta, Hindustan Zinc shares slip 1% after Anil Agarwal-led company confirms ED visit to offices
articles.summary: Shares of Vedanta and Hindustan Zinc fell 1% on June 3 after the Enforcement Directorate visited their offices amid a FEMA investigation. Vedanta confirmed its cooperation with authorities while reiterating its commitment to regulatory compliance.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Hindustan Zinc to Grasim - Jay Thakkar suggests 3 stocks to buy or sell for short-term in F&O segment
articles.summary: The Indian stock market declined on investor caution due to escalating Gulf tensions and rising crude oil prices. Nifty 50 and Sensex opened lower, while analysts expect geopolitical uncertainty and foreign selling to keep equities under pressure in the near term.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO Day 1 LIVE: Issue booked 1.15x so far — GMP signals 33% listing pop. Time to bid?
articles.summary: CMR Green Technologies IPO Day 1 LIVE: CMR Green Technologies IPO sailed through on the first day of bidding today, 3 June. The company enjoys strong grey market premium.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices gain as US-Iran peace deal remains elusive; Brent tops $97/bbl. Where are they headed next?
articles.summary: US-Iran war: MCX crude oil price rose as much as 1% to ₹9,039 per barrel. Here's what's driving the crude oil prices today, along with experts opinion on near-term outlook.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold rate today: MCX gold price rises above ₹1.59 lakh per 10 grams, silver price falls amid renewed US-Iran conflict
articles.summary: Gold rate today: Gold prices declined in the international market, as fresh escalation in the US-Iran war in the Middle East pushed crude oil prices higher, cementing fears that interest rates would stay ​higher for longer to tame inflation.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Silver rate today falls as strong US Jobs Data reinforces higher-for-longer interest rates amid Middle East tensions
articles.summary: Recent US jobs data has strengthened expectations for prolonged elevated interest rates, impacting precious metals. Gold and silver prices are range-bound, recovering from lows while geopolitical tensions in the Middle East keep investors cautious.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rupee opens 19 paise lower at 95.45 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell 19 paise to 95.45 against the US dollar, influenced by rising crude oil prices and Asian currency weakness amid escalating tensions in the Gulf. Investor sentiment weakened due to Iranian military actions and ongoing FPI outflows, despite RBI interventions.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India-US trade deal weeks away as negotiators resolve 99% of issues, says US Ambassador Sergio Gor
articles.summary: US Ambassador Sergio Gor said negotiators have resolved 99% of outstanding issues and expect an interim trade agreement within weeks, with only technical matters left to be finalized.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s services sector maintains growth momentum in May as output expands: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: The HSBC India Services Purchasing Managers’ Index, compiled by S&P Global, rose to 59.8 in May from 58.8 in April. It has continued to grow since April, after registering its weakest growth in 14 months in March.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre’s domestic financing nearly doubles to ₹3.6 trillion in April, easing capital spending pressure
articles.summary: Strong small savings inflows and residual domestic financing provide a cushion for the government's FY27 spending, reducing its reliance on market borrowings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why India's fertility problem may be worse than what headline figures suggest
articles.summary: Driven by changing societal aspirations, India's fertility rate has plunged below replacement levels, particularly in southern states. The desire to have more children is even less.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India GDP growth likely slowed to 7.3% in Q4 FY26: Mint poll
articles.summary: Domestic demand and government spending likely kept growth resilient in the January-March quarter despite headwinds from the West Asia war.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt trims fertilizer demand estimate after IMD downgrades monsoon forecast
articles.summary: Stocks currently stand at 19.98 million tonnes, more than 52% of the revised seasonal requirement, significantly ahead of the typical 33% benchmark at this time in the season.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Industrial output grows 4.9% in April as India shifts to new IIP series with 2022-23 base
articles.summary: The update marks the first official reading under a revised base year, which adds high-tech products and energy-related services to better reflect the modern economy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s manufacturing PMI climbs to 55.0 in May as output hits three-month high
articles.summary: Driven by domestic demand resilience, Indian factory activity outperformed estimates in May as new orders and production grew at their fastest pace since February despite persistent cost pressures.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: GST collections rise 3.2% to ₹1.94 trillion in May
articles.summary: Excluding the impact of a one-time ₹10,000-crore GST payment made by a telecom operator for spectrum allocation in May 2025, gross collections recorded an adjusted growth of 9% during May
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Powell says Fed will lose credibility if president removes officials over policy differences
articles.summary: The remarks were his first since stepping down as Federal Reserve chair.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt to help 150,000 farmers access machinery, curb stubble burning
articles.summary: The government aims to support 150,000 small farmers with ₹8,550 crore for farm equipment access, promoting mechanisation and reducing stubble burning through the establishment of custom hiring centres, enhancing productivity and resource efficiency in agriculture.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rain, thunderstorm seen in many parts, conditions favour monsoon progress: IMD
articles.summary: The IMD has forecast heavy rain in parts of northeast and south India, along with severe thunderstorms, hailstorms and winds up to 90 kmph across several regions. As heatwave conditions ease, southwest monsoon is expected to advance further over Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Lakshadweep and adjoining seas.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Is 2026 heading for its driest monsoon since 2015?
articles.summary: IMD has lowered its monsoon outlook to 90% of normal, raising concerns over heatwaves, farm output, and food prices as El Niño risks loom large.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: How the stress is building in real estate globally, explained in five charts
articles.summary: After soaring in the wake of the covid pandemic, real estate prices globally have been cooling down. The trickle-down effects of the US-Iran war could prolong that.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt warns of fresh inflation risks: Fuel prices, monsoon to further squeeze your household budget
articles.summary: The finance ministry's report comes just days ahead of RBI’s Monetary Policy decision scheduled to be announced next week.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India eases fuel export levies, lowers duties on petrol, diesel and ATF starting June 1
articles.summary: The duty on exports of petrol has been set at ₹1.5 ($0.0158) per litre while that on diesel has been set at ₹13.5 per litre. Export duties on ATF have been set at ₹9.5 per litre.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will AI put economists out of business? Fed’s John Williams says 'I’m not a believer that...'
articles.summary: The implications of artificial intelligence gripped global central bankers in Iceland this week, with one Federal Reserve official joking that it won’t put economists out of business.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Households’ health bills, emerging markets tracker, childcare gap
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India opens CSR funding to non-profits via social stock exchange in landmark policy shift
articles.summary: India government has allowed companies to invest up to 10 per cent of their CSR funds in zero coupon zero principal instruments issued by not for profit organisations through a social stock exchange
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI balance sheet jumps 20.6% on gold rally, rupee depreciation
articles.summary: Liquidity infusion through RBI’s open market purchases of government securities was also a factor for the expansion in the balance sheet
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI says West Asia war clouds growth outlook, but India remains relatively resilient
articles.summary: RBI cautioned that a prolonged escalation of the war could hurt growth momentum and rekindle inflationary pressures through surging crude oil prices, volatile commodity markets and disruptions in shipping routes.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India to get 'plastic notes'? RBI considers decade-old plan. What are polymer banknotes and are they better than paper?
articles.summary: Reserve Bank of India is reportedly reviving a decade-old plan to introduce polymer banknotes, with a pilot project expected soon. Here is what plastic currency is, why the RBI wants it and which countries already use it.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why India is delaying its key Q4 GDP data release by a week
articles.summary: The statistics ministry has shifted the release of Q4 GDP data from 31 May to 7 June each year. This year, the data will be released on 5 June, since 7 June is a Sunday.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: World’s rich pocket $1 billion with big sales of surging stocks
articles.summary: The transactions offer a snapshot into how wealthy private investors are locking in returns in larger positions as global stock indexes hit record highs despite geopolitical turmoil, fueled by enthusiasm for AI and rising corporate earnings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India sees rising evening power shortages as solar supply fades
articles.summary: India's power grid faces challenges as evening demand surges after sunset, leading to widespread outages. A shortage of thermal power and reliance on spot markets contribute to the crisis, particularly in northern and western regions, with demand predicted to rise further due to agricultural needs.
articles.label: BEARISH
